children.text: Preventative health, primary care, chronic disease management
Women’s Health Clinic in New York City

Why go to multiple doctors’ offices—primary care clinic here, gynecologist there—when you can have everything you need at one women’s health clinic in New York City? At Tia, we offer comprehensive, holistic support from compassionate primary care providers, gynecologists, mental health specialists, and wellness practitioners who are here for your whole health.

With locations in the Manhattan (Soho) and Brooklyn (Williamsburg), we’re making it easier for women across New York City to access personalized care that prioritizes your health. Whether you prefer in-person visits or virtual appointments, our team works together to support your mental and physical health—across your whole life.
There’s more than one path to your health

Get whole-body, women-focused care when you need it with Tia Essential, or join as a Tia member for a more personal, supported journey. Whichever path you choose, our commitment to your health and our high-quality care stays the same.
Tia is the only in-person and virtual clinic specifically designed for women across all stages of life

Unlike prescription-only or virtual-only options, we focus on your whole-body health. Unlike your traditional doctor, we’re highly specialized in women’s health. And unlike concierge medicine or other membership-based clinics, we offer a membership-free option. Here’s what you can expect from Tia:
✓ All-in-one care
All of your health needs, including primary care, gyn, labs, acupuncture, and more, all in one place
✓ Women-first, women-focused
High-quality care with highly specialized providers who deeply understand women’s bodies and your unique needs
✓ Fits your lifestyle
Get in-person care if you value that face-to-face, or fit virtual visits into your busy schedule
✓ Options for care
Tia Essential gets you quality care with no membership fee, or join as a Tia member for a more supported experience

Have questions about Tia’s care?
Speak with a member of our Engagement Team at
Why use Tia Essential?

Tia Essential offers a new way to receive Tia’s high-quality, compassionate care without a membership fee. Whether you need a partner for your whole-body health, lightweight support for a well-managed chronic condition, or just reliable care for occasional visits, we've got you covered. This isn't bare-minimum care—it's our signature quality on a "here when you need us" basis, helping you maintain a better baseline of health over time.
Why join Tia as a member?

Our membership option offers a higher level of continuous support, and is designed for women who want greater transparency, personalization, and support throughout their health journey. This option is great for women who want to go deeper into their health or who value the convenience of having someone handle the often messy logistics of healthcare.
The Tia Experience
What will your journey look like?

Everyone’s journey at Tia may look a little different. As a Tia Essential patient, you may come to us first for help with an acute issue like a UTI and then only see us 1x a year. And that’s okay! For Tia Essential patients and Tia members who want to dig a bit deeper into their health, we follow a three-step process that starts you on your
Whole Health Journey
, a holistic model designed to address your immediate concerns and long-term wellness.
Getting started
A three-step process for getting started at Tia
Whether you choose Tia Essential or want to join as a Tia member, getting started is easy:
1. Check your insurance coverage
We’ll validate your insurance details when you sign up for Tia Essential or Tia and will confirm your in-network status before going forward.
2. Schedule your first appointment

Most patients begin with our new patient establishment of care visit to set the foundation for their health journey. During this time, we'll explore your current health concerns, goals, preferences, and health history.
3. Get started on your care journey

For those in need of an annual preventative exam, your provider may recommend this as a first step. If you have different needs, we'll collaborate with you to develop a care plan that works for you and your goals.

faqs.question: Does membership include the cost of appointments?

children.text:
Yes, through our annual Whole Health Exam!

We fuse your gynecology and primary care check-ups (& more!) into a singular, cohesive experience that’s personalized, actionable, and convenient. We intentionally separate your virtual health assessment from your in-person exams and services (delivered at the clinic) so that you get more 1:1 time with your provider, plus additional opportunities for check-ins throughout the year to evaluate and tweak your Care Plan.

As long as you have not had an annual exam or gynecological exam in the past year (we do it all in your Whole Health Exam!) your in-clinic part 2 should be 100% covered by in-network insurance. We accept in-network insurance for part 1 and 3 but you may be responsible for a copay or coinsurance depending on your plan. We always recommend checking with your health plan before booking.
Read more about Tia’s annual Whole Health Exam.

The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story.
We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor and that number climbs to 46% among women ages 18-35,
reporting a negative interaction with a healthcare provider
. This can lead to missed connections between symptoms, overlooked diagnoses, and the frustrating feeling that no one is truly listening.

Why Primary Care matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Women's health isn't just about reproductive organs; it’s about the whole person. A dedicated primary care team helps connect the dots across your health, offering continuous care to understand your full health story.
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health

Building a relationship with a primary care team means catching problems early, managing chronic conditions effectively, and staying healthier long term, instead of just reacting when something feels wrong.
Women’s health deserves specialized care

You’re not imagining it. Women are more susceptible to conditions like migraines and osteoporosis, and hormones influence everything from mood to skin and gut health. Yet, women’s symptoms are often minimized and misdiagnosed. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the whole picture of women's biology and will take your symptoms seriously.

“All women need a home base for their health. So much of traditional healthcare is fragmented, treating each symptom in isolation. At Tia, our providers are trained to connect the dots across your physical, mental, and emotional health. We believe in taking the time to listen your unique story because we know your health isn't a series of disconnected issues—it's a whole picture. Our goal is to empower you with the knowledge to manage your health proactively, helping you not just get well, but stay well."
Emily Rymland, DNP, FNP
, Market Medical Director
How Tia is different for primary care
✓ Hybrid care, built for modern women
In-person and virtual care, so you can choose what works for you. Visit our clinics for hands-on, welcoming care, or schedule virtual visits for quick check-ups or prescription refills from home.
✓ One record, one plan, one team
Every provider you see at Tia works together from a single health record and shared Care Plan, so your care stays connected, not fragmented.
✓ Holistic and comprehensive care
We treat your health as one connected system — integrating mind, body, and hormones to uncover the “why” behind your symptoms and help you feel well from every angle.
✓ Care that grows with you
No need to switch practices as your needs change. Your team supports you though every life stage and health need.

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cramps Or Pelvic Pain

Cramps or pelvic pain can be uncomfortable, disruptive, and hard to ignore. A visit for these symptoms helps uncover what’s going on, whether it’s cycle-related changes, muscle tension, or an underlying condition, and creates a personalized plan to relieve pain and help you feel more in control of your body.
Abnormal Bleeding

From PMS to PCOS, heavy periods to no periods, we can help you evaluate and manage your cycle. Heavy bleeding, clots, and spotting can be symptoms of an underlying issue, and they are never something you have to just "deal with." We'll work together to diagnose what's causing the abnormal bleeding and find a treatment for you. Stop worrying about your cycle and start working toward a healthier, more predictable flow.
Vaginal Infection

From chronic yeast infections to Bacterial Vaginosis (BV) to that "not-sure-what-but-something-is-up" feeling, we've got you covered with diagnosis, treatment, and prevention. We know these symptoms are frustrating, often recurrent, and rarely discussed in a supportive setting. At Tia, we provide expert care to find the root cause, address imbalances, and create a personalized plan to get you back to feeling comfortable and confident, without judgment.

Why Mental Health matters for women
The Connection is Read: Your Mind
is
Your Body

You deserve to feel like yourself again. The belief that mental health and physical health are separate has led to a fragmented system where patients are often left to piece together care on their own. This is frustrating, especially since the impact of mental health on chronic conditions like heart disease, diabetes, and stroke is significant and preventable. Our model is designed to support you by acknowledging that your mental, physical, and reproductive health are inextricably linked.
When “Just Stressed” Gets Overlooked
Women experience anxiety and depression at nearly
twice

the rate of men, yet stress and mood changes are often hard to fully assess in today’s fragmented healthcare system. Key contributors, like hormones, sleep, pain, and life context, can go unrecognized, especially when national depression screening rates in primary care hover around 39%.
Our integrated approach is designed to make it easier to catch what’s really going on. That’s why over
of our patients receive evidence-based depression screening.
A Model Build for the Long Term

Mental health care shouldn't be limited to just one solution. Tia's integrated model is designed to empower you with personalized care, offering a full suite of options from clinical to subclinical modalities—like medication, acupuncture, or supplements. By connecting your primary care with specialized psychiatric support when needed, we ensure you have a collaborative Care Team providing a personalized path toward feeling like your whole self again.e.

“We're here to support our patients in making the decisions that are right for them. Whatever you’re going through, I want you to know: Tia is here to support you feeling better—physically and emotionally. Because improved mental health is improved health.”
Jessica Horwitz, Chief Clinical Officer

children.text: The Problem

Your skin is the largest organ in your body, yet its care is often siloed and disconnected from your overall health. You may wait months for a dermatologist when your issue, like acne or eczema, could be managed sooner by a primary care team with specialized training. Plus, navigating the world of aesthetic treatments feels overwhelming when you just want trustworthy guidance on what’s right for
your
body.
Tia’s Solution

Tia is your home for whole-person skin health. Your skin is a window into your well-being. By connecting conditions like acne and eczema to deeper issues like hormones or stress, we help you treat the root cause. Your care is delivered by an experienced team of primary care and aesthetic providers trained in women’s health, medical dermatology, and aesthetics. This means you get seamless support for both medical skin concerns and aesthetic goals (like wrinkle care or peels) , all in one connected place, without judgment or upselling.
Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s Skin Care may be right for you if:
You want to feel comfortable and confident in your skin.
You're dealing with a chronic condition like acne, eczema, or rosacea that won't go away.
You've noticed suspicious moles or new, unexplained skin changes.
You're interested in Botox or chemical peels but need a safe, expert provider.
You have skin flare-ups and suspect stress, diet, or hormones are to blame.
You want a personalized skin routine that is guided by science, not just social media trends.
You need a provider to check your skin but don't want to wait months for a dermatologist.

Why Skin Care matters
Skin is a window into your whole health

Your skin is the largest organ in your body, and changes often reflect what's happening internally. Conditions like acne, rosacea, and eczema can be flares of deeper issues, including hormonal shifts (like those from PCOS or perimenopause) , stress, or gut health. At Tia, we look past the surface to treat the full picture, ensuring your skin health is never disconnected from the rest of your well-being.
You deserve care that connects the dots

Traditional, fragmented healthcare forces you to treat symptoms in isolation—a dermatologist for your rash, a gynecologist for your hormones, and a PCP for your stress. This disconnect often leads to band-aid fixes and frustration. Building a relationship with a single, connected care team at Tia means we understand how your sleep, mood, and cycle influence your skin, allowing us to find the root cause and build a unified, effective plan.
Skip the long waits and get trusted answers

Tia’s licensed providers are trained to evaluate and treat a wide range of common skin conditions, like acne, rashes, eczema, and more. Instead of enduring a month’s long waitlist for a specialist, you get timely access to medical and aesthetic treatments—from yearly skin exams to clinical wrinkle care—all delivered in a safe, judgment-free environment.
How Skin Care at Tia is different:
Credible Skin Care, Connected to Whole Health:

Unlike Med-Spas or Derm Clinics, Tia integrates medical skin care (like treating acne, eczema, and rashes) with aesthetic services (like wrinkle care and peels) under one roof. Your skin plan works with your primary care, hormones, and overall well-being.
Expert-Led Aesthetic & Medical Care:

Our team includes Licensed medical providers who are specially trained in the services they deliver—from performing skin biopsies to administering neurotoxins, like Botox. This ensures safe, evidence-based treatments you can trust.
One Stop for Convenience and Confidence:

You get a full spectrum of services from an Annual Skin Check to Clinical Wrinkle Care, eliminating the need to visit multiple, disconnected providers. We create a personalized plan designed for confidence-boosting results.
Insurance Accepted for Medical Needs:

Medical dermatology appointments, like treating infections or performing cryotherapy, are billed to your insurance. You can get care for conditions like acne, rosacea, or rashes from your primary care team, helping you skip the long specialist wait times.

children.text: Consider acupuncture if you're:
Looking to support your hormones or hormone-related issues including PCOS, menopause, perimenopause, or cramping
Seeking relief from stress and/or support for anxiety or mood imbalances
Exploring alternatives for pain management for migraines, chronic pain, muscle soreness, or after a procedure like IUD insertion
Trying to conceive or seeking support on your pregnancy journey
Trust the science, the benefits are real
More than 2 out of 3 Tia members see improvement in their symptoms*

The intent of acupuncture is to bring the body back into a state of homeostasis and ignite the body’s innate ability to heal–and that can take some time. It often takes up to 6 treatments within a 3 month window to see significant results.
*Based on self reported data from N=120 Tia acupuncture patients who have had 6 or more appointments in the last 3 months.
What to expect when you try Acupuncture at Tia
The Environment
Relax and let everything melt away. Acupuncture at Tia is done in a semi-private room surrounded by curtains and supported by calming sounds and mood lighting.
The Appointment

Your 60-minute session is divided into two parts: You'll spend the first 20-minutes with your provider discussing your symptoms, goals and progress. Once you’ve aligned, your provider will get into the treatment. You'll spend the remaining 40-minutes in a deep state of relaxation, while the needles do their work.
Check our price list.
Find Acupuncture Services Near You
Meet our Acupuncturists

When you come to Tia for acupuncture, your acupuncturist will work in collaboration with your primary care providers, OB/GYN, and therapist. From two-part appointments like IUD insertion and acupuncture to offering holistic support for fertility, we believe in connected care that treats the whole you.
Why our members love Tia

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cramps Or Pelvic Pain

Cramps or pelvic pain can be uncomfortable, disruptive, and hard to ignore. A visit for these symptoms helps uncover what’s going on, whether it’s cycle-related changes, muscle tension, or an underlying condition, and creates a personalized plan to relieve pain and help you feel more in control of your body.
Abnormal Bleeding

From PMS to PCOS, heavy periods to no periods, we can help you evaluate and manage your cycle. Heavy bleeding, clots, and spotting can be symptoms of an underlying issue, and they are never something you have to just "deal with." We'll work together to diagnose what's causing the abnormal bleeding and find a treatment for you. Stop worrying about your cycle and start working toward a healthier, more predictable flow.
Vaginal Infection

From chronic yeast infections to Bacterial Vaginosis (BV) to that "not-sure-what-but-something-is-up" feeling, we've got you covered with diagnosis, treatment, and prevention. We know these symptoms are frustrating, often recurrent, and rarely discussed in a supportive setting. At Tia, we provide expert care to find the root cause, address imbalances, and create a personalized plan to get you back to feeling comfortable and confident, without judgment.

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Gynecological and sexual health is too often siloed, forcing you to go to separate providers for your annual exam, a painful sex consult, or a new birth control prescription. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story. We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly
one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor.

Why Gynecological and Sexual Health matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Gynecological symptoms aren't isolated; they’re often signals from the rest of your body. A dedicated care team helps connect the dots, understanding how stress, sleep, and overall health can influence things like your period or pelvic pain, sexual wellness, or irregular bleeding.
Your symptoms deserve to be taken seriously.

You're not imagining it. For too long, women's pain—from endometriosis to chronic pelvic issues—has been dismissed and minimized. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the full picture of women's reproductive and sexual health who will listen actively and never brush off your concerns
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health.

Building a relationship with a trusted primary care team means catching problems early, from getting your cervical cancer screening (Pap test) on time to addressing changes in your reproductive health , ensuring you stay healthier for the long term.

"There is no question too personal, too complicated, or too embarrassing when it comes to your gynecologic and sexual health. Our primary role is to be a compassionate, non-judgmental partner in your total well-being."

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cold or Flu Like Symptoms

Feeling run down, congested, or achy and not sure if it’s just a cold or something more? A visit for cold and flu symptoms helps identify what’s going on, ease discomfort, and create a personalized plan so you can recover faster and get back to feeling like yourself.
Ear Issues

Experiencing ear pain, pressure, ringing, or changes in hearing? A visit for ear issues helps uncover what’s causing your symptoms and provides a personalized plan to relieve discomfort, restore balance, and help you feel like yourself again.
Sleep Disturbances

Having trouble falling asleep, staying asleep, or waking up feeling unrested? A visit for sleep disturbances helps uncover what’s disrupting your rest and creates a personalized plan to improve sleep quality, restore energy, and help you feel like yourself again.
Stomach Pain & Issues

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story.
We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor and that number climbs to 46% among women ages 18-35,
reporting a negative interaction with a healthcare provider
. This can lead to missed connections between symptoms, overlooked diagnoses, and the frustrating feeling that no one is truly listening.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right primary care doctor and book your first visit.

children.text: Takes 3–5 minutes to create an account • No membership fee to book • Insurance accepted
Tia’s Solution

We believe women deserve care that treats the whole person. Our primary care gives women one connected place for their whole health,from a cold to chronic conditions. Our providers take time to understand your physical, mental, and emotional health to create a personalized plan built around you.
Our approach helps women live healthier, more vibrant lives:
70% of Tia’s members hadn’t had a preventative exam in the year before joining Tia. After joining,
they complete life-saving screenings at rates 2-3x the national average.
88.2% of Tia patients were screened for depression, nearly
double the national benchmark
because we see mental health as a core part of your health.
It's healthcare that's proactive, not just reactive, designed to help you get well and stay well for the long term.

Why Primary Care matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Women's health isn't just about reproductive organs; it’s about the whole person. A dedicated primary care team helps connect the dots across your health, offering continuous care to understand your full health story.
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health

Building a relationship with a primary care team means catching problems early, managing chronic conditions effectively, and staying healthier long term, instead of just reacting when something feels wrong.
Women’s health deserves specialized care

You’re not imagining it. Women are more susceptible to conditions like migraines and osteoporosis, and hormones influence everything from mood to skin and gut health. Yet, women’s symptoms are often minimized and misdiagnosed. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the whole picture of women's biology and will take your symptoms seriously.

“All women need a home base for their health. So much of traditional healthcare is fragmented, treating each symptom in isolation. At Tia, our providers are trained to connect the dots across your physical, mental, and emotional health. We believe in taking the time to listen your unique story because we know your health isn't a series of disconnected issues—it's a whole picture. Our goal is to empower you with the knowledge to manage your health proactively, helping you not just get well, but stay well."
Emily Rymland, DNP, FNP
, Market Medical Director
How Tia is different for primary care
✓ Hybrid care, built for modern women
In-person and virtual care, so you can choose what works for you. Visit our clinics for hands-on, welcoming care, or schedule virtual visits for quick check-ups or prescription refills from home.
✓ One record, one plan, one team
Every provider you see at Tia works together from a single health record and shared Care Plan, so your care stays connected, not fragmented.
✓ Holistic and comprehensive care
We treat your health as one connected system — integrating mind, body, and hormones to uncover the “why” behind your symptoms and help you feel well from every angle.
✓ Care that grows with you
No need to switch practices as your needs change. Your team supports you though every life stage and health need.

children.text: The Problem

Mental healthcare can feel fragmented, separating your mind from your body. When you feel overwhelmed, anxious, or like "something is off," you may be told it's "just stress" without a look at your hormones, sleep, digestion, or other causes. This leaves you managing symptoms without understanding
why
you feel this way, especially problematic for women who face disproportionately high rates of anxiety and depression compared to men.

items.signupSubcopy: We need some basic info to find you the right weight & metabolic health doctor and book your first visit.

Tia’s Solution for Women

Our mental health care starts with treating your body, not just your brain. Our expert-led whole-person approach looks at the full picture of your health—including the physiological effects of stress, hormones, and sleep—to find the root cause. You get a connected, relationship-based approach with an expanded toolkit, including care from specialty trained primary care providers and psychiatric specialists if needed. We consider mental health a core part of your physical health, ensuring consistent, holistic, and long-term support.

Feeling anxious or overwhelmed all the time
Noticing symptoms of depression or sadness that won't lift
Feeling chronic stress, burnout, or irritability
Struggling with sleep issues or brain fog that are affecting your daily life
Experiencing hormone-related mood changes or feeling "off" after a life stage like postpartum
Navigating a challenging life stage
Curious about exploring whole-body treatment options, including labs, medication, or acupuncture

Why Mental Health matters for women
The Connection is Read: Your Mind
is
Your Body

You deserve to feel like yourself again. The belief that mental health and physical health are separate has led to a fragmented system where patients are often left to piece together care on their own. This is frustrating, especially since the impact of mental health on chronic conditions like heart disease, diabetes, and stroke is significant and preventable. Our model is designed to support you by acknowledging that your mental, physical, and reproductive health are inextricably linked.
When “Just Stressed” Gets Overlooked
Women experience anxiety and depression at nearly
twice

the rate of men, yet stress and mood changes are often hard to fully assess in today’s fragmented healthcare system. Key contributors, like hormones, sleep, pain, and life context, can go unrecognized, especially when national depression screening rates in primary care hover around 39%.
Our integrated approach is designed to make it easier to catch what’s really going on. That’s why over
of our patients receive evidence-based depression screening.
A Model Build for the Long Term

Mental health care shouldn't be limited to just one solution. Tia's integrated model is designed to empower you with personalized care, offering a full suite of options from clinical to subclinical modalities—like medication, acupuncture, or supplements. By connecting your primary care with specialized psychiatric support when needed, we ensure you have a collaborative Care Team providing a personalized path toward feeling like your whole self again.e.

“We're here to support our patients in making the decisions that are right for them. Whatever you’re going through, I want you to know: Tia is here to support you feeling better—physically and emotionally. Because improved mental health is improved health.”
Jessica Horwitz, Chief Clinical Officer
How Tia is different for Mental Health
Tia's approach treats your mental well-being as a core part of your physical health, not something separate.
✓ We Connect Mind, Body, and Hormones

Our providers are specialty trained to look at the whole-system effects of stress across your sleep, digestion, hormones, and more. To ensure we get to the root of your experience, together we may consider additional labs and testing for your unique situation.
✓ An Expanded, Expert-Led Toolkit

You get comprehensive care from specialty trained primary care providers, backed by psychiatric mental health nurse practitioners as needed. Your plan offers a wide range of solutions beyond medication—from acupuncture and supplements to holistic planning and nutrition counseling.
✓ Support Designed for Women's Life Stages

We understand that anxiety and depression rates are disproportionately high for women and often surface during major life transitions. We offer care for women navigating hormonal shifts, postpartum recovery, and perimenopause, providing consistent follow-up and long-term support.

children.text: Primary care, gynecology, mental health services offered in Scottsdale
Insurances accepted at Tia Scottsdale
About Tia Scottsdale

Located near Kierland Commons, Tia Scottsdale is a vibrant, inviting space that celebrates women’s health. With a modern, clean design and popular services like Acupuncture and virtual therapy, the Tia Scottsdale office is a one-stop-shop for women's healthcare.

cells: Additional Information
Where to find us
Our entrance is next to the Sundance store on the Main St side. Enter through the double glass doors with the sign that says “15051 OFFICE” and head to the 2nd floor.
Where to park
Give yourself time to find parking! There is free street parking throughout Kierland Commons and a free parking lot behind Chico's.
When to arrive to the clinic before your appointment

Please arrive to your appointment 15 min early, in case you get lost or have trouble with traffic or parking. If you are late to your appointment, we may not be able to care for you and you may be charged a fee.

children.text: Why choose Tia Scottsdale?
An integrative Care Team specializing in women's health
We employ the highest quality providers that are vetted & trained in whole-person health with a focus on root cause diagnosis & lifestyle-based interventions
Healthcare whenever & wherever you need it
Virtual and in-person appointments on your schedule, Rx refills on the go & easy referrals to top specialists.
Personalized care with thoughtful touches
Tia takes the guesswork & coldness out of healthcare with dedicated Care Coordinators to decode bills & get you in-network referrals to clinics that feel warm & comfortable.
Scottsdale loves Tia

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Cramps Or Pelvic Pain

Cramps or pelvic pain can be uncomfortable, disruptive, and hard to ignore. A visit for these symptoms helps uncover what’s going on, whether it’s cycle-related changes, muscle tension, or an underlying condition, and creates a personalized plan to relieve pain and help you feel more in control of your body.
Abnormal Bleeding

From PMS to PCOS, heavy periods to no periods, we can help you evaluate and manage your cycle. Heavy bleeding, clots, and spotting can be symptoms of an underlying issue, and they are never something you have to just "deal with." We'll work together to diagnose what's causing the abnormal bleeding and find a treatment for you. Stop worrying about your cycle and start working toward a healthier, more predictable flow.
Vaginal Infection

From chronic yeast infections to Bacterial Vaginosis (BV) to that "not-sure-what-but-something-is-up" feeling, we've got you covered with diagnosis, treatment, and prevention. We know these symptoms are frustrating, often recurrent, and rarely discussed in a supportive setting. At Tia, we provide expert care to find the root cause, address imbalances, and create a personalized plan to get you back to feeling comfortable and confident, without judgment.

children.text: Integrated women’s health, all in one place
The Problem

Healthcare should be a relationship, not a series of disconnected transactions. But all too often, it's not. Gynecological and sexual health is too often siloed, forcing you to go to separate providers for your annual exam, a painful sex consult, or a new birth control prescription. Many women feel isolated and alone, passed from one specialist to the next without a central provider who truly knows their entire health story. We know these frustrations are real. In a 2022 report, nearly
one in three women said they felt dismissed by their doctor.

We believe women deserve care that treats the whole person. Our model ensures your gynecology issues are never treated in isolation. Our providers are trained to connect the dots between symptoms like irregular periods or painful sex and your overall physical, mental, and emotional health. This allows us to offer holistic, trauma-informed care that gets to the root cause of conditions like painful sex, irregular bleeding, chronic pelvic pain . These symptoms often connect back to hormones or stress. Our providers don’t just prescribe birth control; we help you find the option that best fits your body, values, and lifestyle.
This approach helps women get comprehensive care, covering all aspects of their well-being:

Mental Health is GYN Health

because we recognize the deep connection between chronic pain, stress, and mental health.

Why Gynecological and Sexual Health matters for women
Your body is a connected system.

Gynecological symptoms aren't isolated; they’re often signals from the rest of your body. A dedicated care team helps connect the dots, understanding how stress, sleep, and overall health can influence things like your period or pelvic pain, sexual wellness, or irregular bleeding.
Your symptoms deserve to be taken seriously.

You're not imagining it. For too long, women's pain—from endometriosis to chronic pelvic issues—has been dismissed and minimized. You deserve care from providers trained to understand the full picture of women's reproductive and sexual health who will listen actively and never brush off your concerns
Prevention is the foundation of lifelong health.

Building a relationship with a trusted primary care team means catching problems early, from getting your cervical cancer screening (Pap test) on time to addressing changes in your reproductive health , ensuring you stay healthier for the long term.

"There is no question too personal, too complicated, or too embarrassing when it comes to your gynecologic and sexual health. Our primary role is to be a compassionate, non-judgmental partner in your total well-being."
Rebecca Reilly, DNP, FNP-C
, Market Medical Director

We are your health home for every stage—from your first Pap test to navigating menopause and beyond.

Why Mental Health matters for women
The Connection is Read: Your Mind
is
Your Body

You deserve to feel like yourself again. The belief that mental health and physical health are separate has led to a fragmented system where patients are often left to piece together care on their own. This is frustrating, especially since the impact of mental health on chronic conditions like heart disease, diabetes, and stroke is significant and preventable. Our model is designed to support you by acknowledging that your mental, physical, and reproductive health are inextricably linked.
When “Just Stressed” Gets Overlooked
Women experience anxiety and depression at nearly
twice

the rate of men, yet stress and mood changes are often hard to fully assess in today’s fragmented healthcare system. Key contributors, like hormones, sleep, pain, and life context, can go unrecognized, especially when national depression screening rates in primary care hover around 39%.
Our integrated approach is designed to make it easier to catch what’s really going on. That’s why over
of our patients receive evidence-based depression screening.
A Model Build for the Long Term

Mental health care shouldn't be limited to just one solution. Tia's integrated model is designed to empower you with personalized care, offering a full suite of options from clinical to subclinical modalities—like medication, acupuncture, or supplements. By connecting your primary care with specialized psychiatric support when needed, we ensure you have a collaborative Care Team providing a personalized path toward feeling like your whole self again.e.

“We're here to support our patients in making the decisions that are right for them. Whatever you’re going through, I want you to know: Tia is here to support you feeling better—physically and emotionally. Because improved mental health is improved health.”
Jessica Horwitz, Chief Clinical Officer

Tia's approach treats your mental well-being as a core part of your physical health, not something separate.

✓ Support Designed for Women's Life Stages

We understand that anxiety and depression rates are disproportionately high for women and often surface during major life transitions. We offer care for women navigating hormonal shifts, postpartum recovery, and perimenopause, providing consistent follow-up and long-term support.

children.text: Symptoms we can help with
Acne, Rash or Skin Changes

Struggling with acne, rashes, or other skin changes that affect your comfort or confidence? A visit for skin issues helps uncover what’s driving your symptoms and creates a personalized plan to calm irritation, support healing, and help you feel like yourself again.

modules.title: Where you can get dedicated care

children.text: The Problem

Your skin is the largest organ in your body, yet its care is often siloed and disconnected from your overall health. You may wait months for a dermatologist when your issue, like acne or eczema, could be managed sooner by a primary care team with specialized training. Plus, navigating the world of aesthetic treatments feels overwhelming when you just want trustworthy guidance on what’s right for
your
body.
Tia’s Solution

Tia is your home for whole-person skin health. Your skin is a window into your well-being. By connecting conditions like acne and eczema to deeper issues like hormones or stress, we help you treat the root cause. Your care is delivered by an experienced team of primary care and aesthetic providers trained in women’s health, medical dermatology, and aesthetics. This means you get seamless support for both medical skin concerns and aesthetic goals (like wrinkle care or peels) , all in one connected place, without judgment or upselling.

Why Skin Care matters
Skin is a window into your whole health

Your skin is the largest organ in your body, and changes often reflect what's happening internally. Conditions like acne, rosacea, and eczema can be flares of deeper issues, including hormonal shifts (like those from PCOS or perimenopause) , stress, or gut health. At Tia, we look past the surface to treat the full picture, ensuring your skin health is never disconnected from the rest of your well-being.
You deserve care that connects the dots

Traditional, fragmented healthcare forces you to treat symptoms in isolation—a dermatologist for your rash, a gynecologist for your hormones, and a PCP for your stress. This disconnect often leads to band-aid fixes and frustration. Building a relationship with a single, connected care team at Tia means we understand how your sleep, mood, and cycle influence your skin, allowing us to find the root cause and build a unified, effective plan.
Skip the long waits and get trusted answers

Tia’s licensed providers are trained to evaluate and treat a wide range of common skin conditions, like acne, rashes, eczema, and more. Instead of enduring a month’s long waitlist for a specialist, you get timely access to medical and aesthetic treatments—from yearly skin exams to clinical wrinkle care—all delivered in a safe, judgment-free environment.
How Skin Care at Tia is different:
Credible Skin Care, Connected to Whole Health:

Unlike Med-Spas or Derm Clinics, Tia integrates medical skin care (like treating acne, eczema, and rashes) with aesthetic services (like wrinkle care and peels) under one roof. Your skin plan works with your primary care, hormones, and overall well-being.
Expert-Led Aesthetic & Medical Care:

Our team includes Licensed medical providers who are specially trained in the services they deliver—from performing skin biopsies to administering neurotoxins, like Botox. This ensures safe, evidence-based treatments you can trust.
One Stop for Convenience and Confidence:

You get a full spectrum of services from an Annual Skin Check to Clinical Wrinkle Care, eliminating the need to visit multiple, disconnected providers. We create a personalized plan designed for confidence-boosting results.
Insurance Accepted for Medical Needs:

Medical dermatology appointments, like treating infections or performing cryotherapy, are billed to your insurance. You can get care for conditions like acne, rosacea, or rashes from your primary care team, helping you skip the long specialist wait times.

children.text: Consider acupuncture if you're:
Looking to support your hormones or hormone-related issues including PCOS, menopause, perimenopause, or cramping
Seeking relief from stress and/or support for anxiety or mood imbalances
Exploring alternatives for pain management for migraines, chronic pain, muscle soreness, or after a procedure like IUD insertion
Trying to conceive or seeking support on your pregnancy journey
Trust the science, the benefits are real
More than 2 out of 3 Tia members see improvement in their symptoms*

The intent of acupuncture is to bring the body back into a state of homeostasis and ignite the body’s innate ability to heal–and that can take some time. It often takes up to 6 treatments within a 3 month window to see significant results.
*Based on self reported data from N=120 Tia acupuncture patients who have had 6 or more appointments in the last 3 months.
What to expect when you try Acupuncture at Tia
The Environment
Relax and let everything melt away. Acupuncture at Tia is done in a semi-private room surrounded by curtains and supported by calming sounds and mood lighting.
The Appointment

Your 60-minute session is divided into two parts: You'll spend the first 20-minutes with your provider discussing your symptoms, goals and progress. Once you’ve aligned, your provider will get into the treatment. You'll spend the remaining 40-minutes in a deep state of relaxation, while the needles do their work.
Check our price list.
Find Acupuncture Services Near You
Meet our Acupuncturists

When you come to Tia for acupuncture, your acupuncturist will work in collaboration with your primary care providers, OB/GYN, and therapist. From two-part appointments like IUD insertion and acupuncture to offering holistic support for fertility, we believe in connected care that treats the whole you.

children.text: Gabriela, where are you from?

I was born and raised in Miami, FL and came to NYC after graduating from college to dive into the city of opportunity. I have always dreamed of experiencing the dynamic culture and diversity that NYC is known for.
What is most challenging about navigating healthcare in NYC?

As a woman, I have personally found navigating healthcare in NYC overwhelming in terms of finding consistent primary and gynecological care. After working in healthcare for 5 years, I continually find that my patients are plagued with the same fear and anxiety when looking for quality care that is uniquely tailored to women.
What does it mean to be a “well-woman” in 2019?

To me, being a “well-woman” in 2019 means having consistent access to comprehensive care that addresses all of the unique needs of a female. Women should not be treated by the same criteria as men, and cycle-connected care needs to be an integral component in treating women.
What’s the most important thing you’ve learned about yourself over the last decade?

I have learned the importance of maintaining balance in all aspects of life, and how necessary it is to put self care first. I used to always put other people’s needs before my own, which limited my personal growth and left me no time for myself.
If you could go back in time, what do you wish you knew about your health that you know now?
I wish I had known about the negative impact that stress and anxiety took on my health, and the importance of being introspective and practicing mindfulness throughout the day.
What’s your favorite way to self-care?
Meditation, hiking, and relaxing at the beach / all things ocean-related.
Why did you join the Tia Clinic?
I joined because I believe in the importance of the Tia mission of female empowerment, and to become a part of the movement encouraging women to take back control over their health.
What is your definition of optimal female health that you hope to achieve with support from Tia?
My definition of optimal female health is having all medical needs met. With the help of Tia I aim to help guide patients through a visual narrative on their path towards optimal health.
What drew you to ultrasound?

I’ve always been a very visual person and had a curiosity for the practice of medicine. The complexity of the human body fascinated me from a young age and I constantly found myself analyzing how our organs work with one another. I had an ultrasound done of my abdomen when I was in high school and I was awestruck. Peering into the human body in real-time, I knew I had to become an Ultrasound Technologist.
Why is it a powerful tool for the betterment of women’s health (beyond just pregnancy)?

Ultrasound is a powerful tool for the betterment of women’s health because it gives insight into so many different organ systems beyond just reproductive. From gastrointestinal to endocrine and cardiovascular we can evaluate a range of organ systems that impact women’s optimal health. Ultrasound gives us the ability to assess the vessels, liver, gallbladder, kidneys, thyroid-and the list goes on.
Providing comprehensive care means evaluating connections between all of these organ systems.
When should a woman discuss ultrasound with her doctor?
If you are experiencing any of these things, you should discuss ultrasound as a potential diagnostic tool with your doctor to learn more about why:
Painful sex
Painful periods
Heavy, irregular periods or vaginal bleeding
Pelvic pain or bloating
Continual pain or irregular bleeding with presence of IUD
Back or flank pain (mid-lower back at the site of kidneys)
Blood in urine or chronic UTIs
Pain in upper right abdomen (or radiating right shoulder pain)
Persistent vomiting associated with abdominal pain
Leg pain or swelling/ redness
Thyroid enlargement
Redness/ swelling or palpable lump of a soft tissue
Are there any specific things ultrasound can be used to help diagnose?
Yes! It can be used as a diagnostic to help with….
Reproductive issues like:
PCOS
Endometriosis of the Uterus or Ovaries
Fibroids
Ovarian Cysts/Tumors
Endometrial Polyps
Endometrial carcinoma
Hydrosalpinx (fluid in the Fallopian tubes)
Correct IUD Placements
Uterine malformations
Endocrine issues like:
Thyroid nodules
Thyroid conditions: Hypothyroidism & Hyperthyroidism
Salivary Glands (Parotiditis)
Abdominal issues like:
Gallstones/ Gallbladder and Biliary disease
Liver Disease
Kidney Disease (stones, infection, obstruction)
Appendix
Vascular issues like:
Blood Clots
What’s unique about doing ultrasound as part of an integrative care team vs. working at a hospital?

At Tia, I have the opportunity to be an extension of the providers’ care and vice versa. This means providing on-the-spot diagnoses and relaying integral information to the care team. The hospital setting did not foster this type of environment, as my exams were isolated. This made it difficult to establish continual patient relationships. Now, I have the ability to follow patients progress over time and be a collaborative member of the Tia care team every step of the way!

children.text: Last week we hosted our very first Tia Tackles event —
Dr. Terri-Ann Bennett

shared her wisdom and expertise in conversation with Dr. Stephanie McClellan, Chief Medical Officer of Tia. They spoke about the need for patient advocacy for BIPOC, what patient advocates do, and how to weave them through the clinical experience from institutional change to providers to the layperson. Here is some of what we learned.

The American healthcare system has a long legacy of racism and discrimination, fuel for the disparity that is pervasive in this system. Navigating the healthcare system is daunting for most, but especially for BIPOC. Due to an array of access barriers, BIPOC not only receive less care than white people but also receive worse care. BIPOC are disproportionately misdiagnosed, under-diagnosed, and mistreated, quality of care directly correlates to worse health outcomes.

Emotional expressions and experience of pain by Black individuals are more likely to be misperceived. Pain is under-diagnosed and under-treated as a result of racial bias. Stereotyping is ever-present in clinical situations, leading certain populations to emphasize specific class and cultural resources such as cultural health capital (cultural skills, attitudes, behaviors, and interaction styles) to leverage and mitigate discrimination.

Epigenetics, the study of inherited genetic trait changes, is a quickly evolving field showing that trauma and its effects may be passed through generations, recognizable through gene expression and cellular activity that is influenced by external factors such as environment. Inherited trauma may be likened to the permanence of scar, the remnants of the initial wound become a scar inscribed into a person that then passes on through their lineage. Generational trauma can impact livelihood via both the mind and body.

There are social determinants of health that directly impact BIPOC — economic and social conditions, such as living and working conditions (think: the distribution of income, wealth, influence, and power) that influence individual and group differences in health status (think: risk and vulnerability of disease or injury) and access to care.

editorialText.title: How can we create a built-in system for patient advocacy?

children.text: Trust facilitates care-seeking behavior and promotes patient honesty and adherence. Distrust inhibits care-seeking and may lead to non-adherence. One study in Chicago looked at how trustworthy Black people found their physicians. The study found that participants indicated that trust is determined by the interpersonal and technical competence of physicians. Contributing factors to distrust in physicians included a lack of interpersonal and technical competence, perceived quest for profit, and expectations of racism and experimentation during routine health care. Another study looking at race in patient-physician relationships found that the quality of interpersonal care is vital in facilitating a patient’s involvement in their care, increased patient involvement improves patient satisfaction and outcomes.

One way to effectively create institutional change is to promote diversity within the healthcare system. Data suggests that African American patients rate their visits with physicians as less participatory than whites. However, patients seeing physicians of their own race rate their physicians’ decision-making styles as more participatory. Improving cross-cultural communication between physicians and patients and providing patients with access to a diverse group of physicians may lead to more patient involvement in care, higher levels of patient satisfaction, and ultimately, better health outcomes. A study conducted by Stanford looked at Black men in Oakland, found that men seen by Black physicians were more likely to engage with them, and even consent to preventive services like cardiovascular screenings and immunizations. Black doctors were 71% more likely to get their patients to receive a cholesterol screening, whereas non-Black doctors were 45% as likely. The study estimates this approach could reduce the Black-white mortality gap due to heart disease by 19%.

Dr. Terri-Ann Bennett believes that providers should be trained early on in their schooling to understand not only bias but also the actual biological differences that result from inherited trauma. Beyond this training, she recommends that providers continually check their bias while working with patients and actively seek to understand why their patients have fears or misunderstandings. Understanding why gives way to an empathetic response, leading with empathy engenders advocacy. Dr. Bennett suggests implicit bias training but also asks that providers take it a step further by enhancing their cultural competency and actively work to have an anti-racist practice.

Research shows that patient advocacy is effective. One study looking at a sample of 30–74-year-old self-identified African-American and/or Black women who had received abnormal mammogram results found advocacy to be a positive factor in their medical experiences. The participants felt that engaging in medical advocacy helped address ongoing experiences of mistrust and racism in the medical setting. Participants also expressed that they felt accountable for their own bodies because they were involved in decisions about their care because they were informed about their decisions.
Institutions can support patient advocacy by…
Promoting body literacy by actively teaching and making information readily accessible which empowers patients to advocate for themselves.
Personalizing medicine, rather than generalizing.

Revolutionizing healthcare delivery by incorporating patient advocates throughout the patient journey and actively supporting their presence (eg. doulas, friends and family members joining appointments).
Providers can advocate for their patients by…
Empowering them through teaching and shared-decision making.
Reminding patients of their rights.
Using communication styles that support understanding.
Ensuring care continues after appointments (eg. follow-ups, lifestyle changes, medication, etc.).
Patients can advocate for themselves by…
Bringing someone with them (eg. friend or family member) for support. Note: hospital systems and third party agencies are often able to provide advocates to patients.
Not ending an appointment until they feel they understand their provider fully.
Researching before and after visits.

Ask all the questions! Ask why and why again! Ask about side-effects, advantages, and disadvantages of recommendations made by the provider. Pro-tip from Dr. Bennett: come to your appointment with your questions written down.
Familiarizing themselves with the
patient bill of rights
Patient's bill of rights

A patient’s bill of rights is a list of guarantees on patient information, fair treatment, and autonomy over medical decisions for those receiving care. It is usually found in the paperwork you sign upon arrival!
Asking for an interpreter if needed.
SOURCES
Cooper-Patrick, L., Gallo, J. J., Gonzales, J. J., Vu, H. T., Powe, N. R., Nelson, C., & Ford, D. E. (1999). Race, gender, and partnership in the patient-physician relationship.
JAMA

Jacobs, E. A., Rolle, I., Ferrans, C. E., Whitaker, E. E., & Warnecke, R. B. (2006). Understanding African Americans’ views of the trustworthiness of physicians. Journal of general internal medicine, 21(6), 642–647.
Marcella Alsan & Owen Garrick & Grant Graziani, 2019. Does Diversity Matter for Health? Experimental Evidence from Oakland. American Economic Review, vol 109(12), pages 4071–4111.
Mende-Siedlecki, P., Qu-Lee, J., Backer, R., & Van Bavel, J. J. (2019). Perceptual contributions to racial bias in pain recognition.
Journal of experimental psychology. General
https://doi.org/10.1037/xge0000600
Molina, Y., Hempstead, B.H., Thompson-Dodd, J.
et al.
Medical Advocacy and Supportive Environments for African-Americans Following Abnormal Mammograms.
J Canc Educ
Tina K. Sacks (2018) Performing Black womanhood: a qualitative study of stereotypes and the healthcare encounter, Critical Public Health, 28:1, 59–69,

children.text: Interested in joining Tia?
Join Now

One of the most exciting things about Tia’s growth and evolution as a healthcare provider is that we’re continually improving, changing, assessing, and evaluating our services to make sure we’re meeting the needs of our community. We understand the deep history of healthcare in the United States as a site of exclusion, oppression, and trauma for many people, making our commitment to personalized, affirming, whole-person care all the more critical. That also means we have a responsibility to take an intersectional approach to our diversity, equity, and inclusion initiatives at every level of our organization, and to offer transparency about that process to our community. You’ve asked us important questions about who we serve, the line between our values and expertise, and what we’re doing to make Tia a more inclusive provider -- we’re here to provide some answers.
At Tia, we call ourselves the “modern medical home for women”. We understand that solely referencing cisgender women in the context of women’s health excludes a diverse group of
transgender
and
gender nonbinary

people who have healthcare needs and experiences that can be similar to, but also unique from, those of cisgender women.

Trans women are women, full stop. When we reference “women'' at Tia, our language is always inclusive of trans women, who face unique and disproportionate barriers to healthcare, especially if they are women of color.
Trans people and nonbinary individuals are welcome and celebrated at Tia, and are active members of the Tia patient community today.

As a Tia member, your gender identity will be affirmed and respected. Before you ever meet with one of our providers, you’ll fill out a detailed health record form that will tell us important details about your identity, including your gender, pronouns, and sexual orientation, so we can ensure that we are referring to you correctly and asking appropriate questions about your health and life experiences.
More than 7% of Tia patients use a pronoun other than she/her and identify across the gender spectrum
. Where appropriate, we provide our patients clinical context as to why it's important for a provider to understand this information; it’s core to our
care philosophy

, specifically, our commitment to personalized care and treating the person, not (just) the patient. We recognize the multitude of ways that a patient’s lived experience influences their health and access to care: clinically, psychosocially, and experientially. All Tia providers undergo training on inclusive care as well as inclusive language, protocols, and the individualized, connected model of Tia care that centers our patients and gives you full agency over your health care.
Tia provider screen that includes important information about patient identity.

While our community is explicitly open and welcoming to people of all genders, we have a duty to be transparent that we are not a center of excellence for every person across the gender spectrum at this time. Tia offers a range of services from primary care to acupuncture to gynecology and mental health, which are currently utilized by trans and nonbinary patients. We provide primary care services for cis, trans and nonbinary patients (ranging from blood pressure management to acute injury or illnesses) and at this time work with our community specialist partners who provide gender affirming hormone therapy and surgical services. While we do not currently offer these specific services that we know are critical to the trans community, you still have a place at Tia. If you are a Tia member in need of a service that we do not offer, we will work with you one on one to refer you to the high-quality, trusted care you require to live healthily and as your authentic self.

As a healthcare provider, Tia is committed to growing and evolving, always keeping diversity, equity, and inclusion at the heart of our mission. As we look forward to our future, we commit to gender inclusivity in the following ways at this moment:
Creation of a Tia DEI Advisory Council
made up of experts and members to strategize and guide us in becoming a more inclusive health care providers
Engaging an experienced LGBTQ+ consultant,
Shane Snowdon
, to ensure our organization and environment of care (for both employees and patients) supports the health of people of all gender identities and sexual orientations
Continued education for staff and providers on issues in LGBTQ+ health
Community events that specifically speak to the experiences and needs of LGBTQ+ members
Building more partnerships with LGBTQ+ institutions, influencers, and health experts / educators
Expansion of the language we use with regard to gender in our clinic experience and communications
Increasing representation of LGBTQ+ people on the Tia Care Team and our staff as a whole
Providing a listening ear to our community and an open door policy for feedback and ideas for our growth. Reach out at
sayhello@asktia.com

At Tia, we understand that the journey of growth and change can be messy and difficult. We know there may be stumbling blocks or mistakes ahead, and we thank our community for holding us accountable and being a partner in our evolution. Learning is never over, and we’re committed to the process. Our services and care model can grow and change in the future, and we are open to so many possibilities.
We welcome your ideas, questions, and stories of your successes and challenges at every step

children.text: Rebecca is a DNP-trained family nurse practitioner who utilizes an individualized, holistic approach while implementing evidence-based practice to achieve the best possible health outcomes for her patients. Throughout her career, she's worked with patients across the lifespan from a variety of socioeconomic and cultural backgrounds. Passionate about empowering women through healthcare, she provides judgment-free counseling & information that fosters shared decision-making, so each woman has a care plan in place that fits her individual needs.
